Text:
BASED
i think that in its purest and truest sense, "based" is an ontic-semiotic term, a "commendation of being" or mode of self-signification which, eschewing external frames of reference, calls instead upon one's own inmost interiorities, subjectivities, absurdities as origin point (0,0) from whence proceeds the Hyperhuman, grotesque and parabolic. Based.
based on what? on capital A Authenticity (or Anonymity) writ large (or small) with none of the stink of societal coercion, mass opinion, conformity hanging about it. based is the great Diffusion, prismatic. it is the singular infinite (You) giving voice to itself as itself in a language perversely and unflinchingly its own, even and especially when so doing comes at risk of ridicule or general disapprobation.
it's sad, then, that with its growing popularity, the term has been reduced to little more than neoreactionary slang, subcultural shorthand for a particular kind of gleeful contrarianism and incendiary juvenility.
tldr: it's not based to be based, any more

SPIN*

Ken Moore was an appliance salesman, working for the company that shared his name. It seemed too convenient, too unlikely. "Like the washing machines?!" "Any relation?" Yes, like the washing machines. No, no relation.

Ken's numbers were good; he moved units. Not on charm or persistence or any of the usual virtues of his trade, but by a discreet lack of them. 

While his collegeaues stalked the floor, wheedling hapless indecisives, Ken took his mark in one of the little mock display kitchens, quietly perusing an owner's manual. Like a stage actor when the lights come on, suspended in time.

If they want something, they'll come and ask; if they don't they won't.

Best, Ken thought, to let the buyer be. Where he got this philosophy was from a book he read in childhood about a wise old goose and a little gosling. 

The gosling would poke its head through the narrow bars of the gate in the park, saying, "I can fit through, see, I'm as nimble as can be. What do you think of that old goose." The old goose would clear his throat: "the bars are wide as three feathers, no more and no less."

The gosling, of course, became a goose himself, but kept at his silly games. And each time, without a note of remonstrance, would come the old refrain.

Just the facts, the specs. Just declarative statement about the width of the bars and the spaces between. Were there any deals at the moment. Yes, 50% on the HE top loader over there. 2 year warantee. 

Any compact models? Any eco friendly options? Yes, and here they are. And here is what they do. And here is their capacity.

At the end, the careless bird got his head stuck, but that wasn't the point at all. The book was a pangyeric to restraint. It was the greatest thing he had ever read, but he hadn't read much.

PURGATION
"Is this the sort of branch,  asked the small boy to the large one.
He came out of the trees, dragging it like a tail behind him. The grass crunched with frost.
 No, too big.         
The small boy huffed, his breath visible.  We could break it up. 
 No.  
 Why not. 
 Why not. Because.  The large boy was twisting a sapling at its base, a mash of wet fibers.  Hand me that hatchet now.  
The small boy sat down ignoring him, felt the dampness of the ground. 
 Hey,  said the large boy.
 Hey,  said the small one.
In silence, they surveyed their handwork: a crude wetu or domed hut, made of arched sticks and poles. The Wampanoag had built wetu for shelter and for ceremony, impermanent structures of great significance. 
***
Far off across the long low field, smoke rose from the chimneys of the new houses. 
There would be more houses in the field soon, parceled out in grids. Slabs and materials. Hammers clattering off flat surface out into open space.
 Hey,  said the small boy pointing.
A figure was making its uneven way through the grass toward them, disrupting birds.
The large boy squinted, went severely back to work. 
He had seen a sketch of the wetu in a book. It seemed achievable. It was something to make, something to get up early for on a Saturday morning. Simple tools would suffice: a hatchet and some twine. This was how the Indians had probably done it, probably in this spot or general area.
There were footsteps approaching. It was a girl, bundled-up and sniffling.
 Get lost  scowled the large boy. The small boy echoed him, quietly:  get lost. 
She stopped and stared.  Why. 
 Get lost, that s why.  
 What is it  she asked.
 What s what."
She stared vacantly, wiped her raw, dripping nose. 
 What s what  he repeated.
She nodded at the structure. 
 We re trying to build,  the large boy explained.
 I can help you build. 
 We don t want you to. 
 I can bring blankets for the inside. For warmth. 
 We don t want that. 
They looked at each other, the boy and the girl. 
Far off, a truck in reverse. The builders had arrived at the site, with supplies and cigarettes behind their ears. They would be climbing up ladders and nailing down boards.
The large boy tugged suddenly, savagely at the sapling, lost his balance. The girl laughed.
 Get lost, get lost,  he shouted. 
The girl backed away, dodging a clod of dirt. She laughed again.  I m going to get blankets for you. 
 Fuck you  said the large boy, looking around for something else to throw.  Hand me that hatchet. 
The small boy sat there mute, transfixed, watching the girl recede. The rising sun was a disc around her head.  
 Fuck you.  muttered the large boy. He kicked the small boy hard in the stomach, watched him gasp on the sharp wet earth.

Laid out like keys of old pianos
Recital of the small and intimate

Hang a wreath, o daughters of Zion

I heard thin laughter in the fellowship hall
a modest proposal
pinch of the flesh of the arm

She said to me, desperately,  he is wise ;

with knees for prayer and fellatio
and guilt like lipstick discreetly applied

Kiss the napkin, fold it up.
scribbles in margins, near misses
a pack of parliaments in Charleston harbor

(No that isn t quite it 
that isn t how it was in movies
or a photograph)

But I know that she will walk in two years  time
tassels, cardstock, a smile framed and lasting

stuck to my book of phrases
half or barely remembered instances

We set off fireworks in the front yard 
burnt out sparklers
graves the length of flowerbeds

Who will live there
In that house on the hill
A dwelling bright and biblical 

Who will count them,
These long and varnished days
Laid out like keys of old pianos
Recital of the small and intimate

Hang a wreath, o daughters of Zion 
Lay out a mat for Babylon s hordes

I heard thin laughter in the fellowship hall
a modest proposal
pinch of the flesh of the arm

She said to me, desperately,  he is wise ;
she told me he was righteous
with knees for prayer and fellatio
and guilt like lipstick discreetly applied

Kiss the napkin, fold it up.
scribbles in margins, near misses
a pack of parliaments in Charleston harbor

(No that isn t quite it 
that isn t how it was in movies
or a photograph)

But I know that she will walk in two years  time
tassels, cardstock, a smile framed and lasting

Text:
RAIN
I get obsessive when it rains. These storms do a number on me. Small things, petty things, I get hung up. What's the trigger for it? Is it physiological, psychosomatic? There's freight a mile back. 
26 years old. 25. I round up, am hung up. Online takes me in circles, small tight ones that approximate madness. Afraid of that: asphyxia of the mind or lungs. 
Well you watch some TV. You browse. There's work, memories to gnaw on, freight a mile back. "Mold describes parabolas." Nice.
More than one guy has said of language, this or that. The inward turn. Can't-get-at-back-of-it. And that's cycles, that's the whole thing of it. Affluenza, I'm sick.
Opening an umbrella and closing it to get the water off, repeatedly. 
Nice.

INCOMMUNICABLE*
Inside   why? Because we have been given; Outside   what? The power and the glory; Union   how? By bringing together
Where is the center? The godself, now and ever What is the outworking? Being and knowing and loving. To what end? To encircle and expand, tranquil & strong 
What is the purpose of the man? Not to determine but to contemplate the determining, interrogation of the interrogative function itself 
What is love? It is sensing the self in the other. Put another way, it is feeling the patterned sensation of patterned sensation being felt.
This is that which is meant, that which is felt, known, seen: Being, the stuff of the world. A singular pluarity, the monadic manifold. You know this for you have proceeded from it and will return to it and are even now with it, a tessellation or interior corner.

LIFE-ARC*

      Structured play (short fiction, poetry, notebooks upon notebooks -  the Geoffrey era [high school])
      Puritanical efficiency (joyless journal entries, no-nonsense research papers   undergrad)

      Ironic sneering (weird twitter, parodic  scholarship    grad school)

PORCH
In the dark, the father is sitting with his son, two outlines. Everyone knows Orion's belt, but it needs to be pointed out when visible.
"And Polaris, the north star. The sky rotates around that."
The son knows that, swats at a dim bug.
"And Vega. V for Vega. A goose or something. Do you want a cigarette?"
"I'm too young."
"Have one anyway." He holds one out and pulls it back. "Ah, too young, too young."
Other stars he knows are the Dippers, big and small. Ursa Major, Ursa Minor. The morning star, Venus, which can be seen in the early hours.
"Anyway you'll get cancer. I'm your dad and I won't let you smoke."
"I shouldn't smoke."
"You shouldn't smoke." He pantomimes a gasp. "Anyway, look here. I'm your father. It's time I told you about something."
The son looks down. The porchlight comes on and the mother comes out. 
"Hey we're having a moment here." 
She drags over an iron chair screeching and sits down and lights up a cigarette.
"Hey don't mind me."
In the porchlight, in the night, they sit there heavily. They are waiting for something that will not occur, or is unlikely to.

ILLUMINATION
Isaac went door to door at the Remedy Motel handing out pamphlets. It was a grey march day, just off the interstate, a day for trumpets and hoofbeats and ionized particles colliding.
He knocked at Room 117.
There were sounds inside the room, shuffling, but no one answered. Isaac slid out the pen from the bible beneath his arm, clicked it, felt the slack in the mechanism. 
 Kid.  a shout behind him.  Jesus isn t home.  
He turned to see a man with a shopping cart, gaunt and brown, clothes like a loose skin around him.
 Where s Jesus?  the man barked. 
 In my heart, that s what the gospel tells us. 
 Bullshit. You got no mileage.  The man jerked his head at an old Buick, gutted and rusting in the lot.  Well? 
Isaac squinted.  Do you know where you will spend eternity? 
The man threw up his arms.  Not here. 
Isaac watched him shuffle off, pushing his cart along the weedy cracks in the asphalt that ran like green veins toward the overpass. 

Room 118. A face appeared in the window, then a middle finger. Nails long for piercing, stigmata.
119, no one. 
It was  salvageable,  that Buick on blocks. Belonged to the manager who dozed with his feet up in the front office. He was going to fix it up this spring, every spring, and drain the water from the pool, which children caught frogs in.
Placing the pen back in his bible, Isaac decided he would try the second level. Someone was smoking there, leaned against the railing. 
 Have you heard the good news.   qu ?  
200, 201, nothing
Knock on the doors it was ministry then off to the diner across the way and then home. A weekly affair. The diner had a pinball machine you could play on for free, though the left bumper was stuck. 
202
The door opened and a man with a lazy eye looked out, holding a gun.  Yes.  he said.
Isaac stared at the good eye. He pressed his thumb against the pen s protruding nib a dot of ink, a smear on gilded edges.  Hello, would you be able to talk. 
 Nope. Come in. 
He went haltingly into the dim room; the air hung heavy, suspense of motion. Andy Griffith was on the tv, turned down.  Isaac made out three others in the glow: a tall figure in the corner, two shapes sprawled on the bed. 
 Who- 
 Whores,  said the lazy eye, pointing with the gun.  Have a seat there.  
Isaac took the proffered chair, slacks riding high on white socks and leg. It was one where Opie steals, he d never seen it.  Well, have you heard the good news. 
 Sure, you could say that.  
A reedy chuckle from the corner.
 The good news is this. Well, the bad news is this: you are a sinner. 
 Not a chance. 
One of the whores coughed, sounds of stirring.
 But there is good news and a way out. I can show you.  He opened his bible to the pen.
 Don't show me that shit. I don t need to see it.  
Isaac pointed at a verse.  But don t you see. 
 He doesn t need to,  rasped the figure in the corner.
 The Scripture is clear. The truth is plain. For God so loved the world. 
 Nope,  said the lazy eye, his gaze half-wandering: an errant son, lawman s crisis in greyscale.
 Whosoever shall call on the name-   Nope   ..whosoever shall call, he shall be saved.  
 Wrong. there s more to it.   he went over to a drawer, lazy eye, pulled out a length of rope.
 The wages of sin is death.  continued Isaac, smoothing the pages, annotations like braille.  The Bible says that. 
 Uh huh."
 Take some literature. Come to church. 
 That s right, keep talking.  lazy eye circled his proselyte, working the rope around him.  Go on, go on. 
 Yes go on,  metallic laughter. A whore fell off the bed and moaned. 
 He is coming again,  said Isaac, tightening.  To judge the living and the dead.  
There was a knock. 
A moment s intake, shh. Then the scramble, the skitter, dark objects rustling into closets and corners; the figure pulled a curtain over itself; muttered fucks.
Unperturbed, lazy eye finished his knot then went slowly to the door. Isaac heard it open then a gunshot. 

203, 204. The sky was alloy steel driving east on the interstate, a low ceiling, a long ringing corridor of silence. Put your quarter in, knock the machine. Tilt sensors, readouts, gobble-holes. Enclosed spaces where things bounced around toward declination.  205.
What was it they said? Mightier than the sword. In the beginning was the Word? Word as work. By your works you shall know them. High score in the book of Life. 
206, 207
This do in remembrance. Contours of the resin barrel, cool metal clip. 208. Click, click.

 You shoot him  asked the man behind the curtain.
 Yeah I shot him,  said the lazy eye. He went to the tv and turned it up. 
What has gotten into you, boy. Gee I dunno Pa. You don t know? Well you re about to know.
Snap of a lighter, smell of burning plastic. 
From their places they all crept over and squatted down around Isaac s chair.  See,  the lazy eye said tapping his head.  You need to think things out. From the bottom up. God? He killed himself and we re the pieces. 
Like a sacrament, they hit the foil, drawing in vapor through a straw passed around. A ceiling fan squeaked rhythmically overhead, whirling the smoke in slow thick strands.

ANIMATION
To what can the uncanny quality of modern animation be attributed? Noodly arms, soft figures, a simplification and rounding out of human form. 
Dance is given prominence spontaneous (anti) choreographies, kinesthetic monosyllables, the inertial flurry.
Gyrational and algorithmic, the celebration iterates on itself, proceeding toward Purchase, a dumb reenactment of half-remembered rituals that no longer signify save as  reference. 
In a state of nauseating lightness, we are swept up, reduced to color and sound, then dropped on our heads, confused and greatly lessened.

OUROBOROS
Ouroboros: An Edenic Theory of Seeming
I want to set out a new terminological framework of Edenic seeming that will clarify/complicate the relationship between the self, the world, and the self's experience of the world--a relationship that I propose is circular, autological, and eternal, rather than linear, heterological, and time-bound. 
I say a terminological framework because its primary contribution is not one of ideological delineation but of grammatical contortion and redefinition, a noun-ing and verb-ing. You could make (and others have made) my central argument--that the self is the world is the experience of the world--without these superficial innovations; nonetheless, I hope my efforts will help cast new and strange light on questions of ontology, metaphysics, and epistemology, particularly as seen in the Biblical account of man's fall from grace: Adam (self) eats (experiences) apple (world). 
Framing
Now to the invention at hand, the neat trick: to grant transitivity where there is none.  
The apple seems good. 
Seems functions here as a linking verb, a qualified is. Implicit in declaring something seems a certain way is the acknowledgement that it may not in fact be that way. For the game's sake, let's (a)void the risk of proprioceptive fallibility.
To seem is henceforth to eat, as Adam does his apple. This is not simply the mechanical eating of jaw and tongue, nor is it the optic eating of packets of light, nor is it the eating of haptic surface or sonic wave. It is all of these eaten together: an active, involuntary consumption of the world as sensation (although, as we will soon see, seeming is as much creation as it is consumption). 
Adam seems the fruit. 
He takes in qualia; he calls them apple. He ought not, but he must. As the proto-self, Adam seems the fate of man, a seeming enacted always and ever. Note here it does not matter that the Apple (from now on used interchangeably with World [the glistening sphere, the seemed object]) may only be a dreaming--all that matters is the seeming. I (read: Adam, self, seemer) do not know the nature of the object I am eating. It may or may not "exist"--world qua World. I can only know this: that I am eating it. What "I" am, what "it" is, what exactly is meant by this "eating" may be debated. But the experiential sensations of my eating are undeniable, self-evident, and known. Seeming seems. 
So what have we done? We have made ourselves eaters and seemers of the World as seemed object. Now, with this definitional frame in place, we turn our attention to the three questions hinted at above: 
What is the seemed (world, apple) 
What is the seemer (self, Adam) 
What is the seeming (experiencing, eating) 
The questions are three, but I propose the answer is one namely, Seeming Eternally Seemed. To demonstrate, I offer a series of thought experiments, each corresponding to a question posed.  
Question 1
For this thought experiment, you must first seem an object. A cup, a book, a grain of salt. You apprehend it, take it in; it is seemed. Now, turn away and--in your mind--return the object to its unseemed state. By this I do not mean simply forgetting the object or "cropping it out." To unseem the object, you must imagine it was never seemed. Can you do it? No. For if you unseem the object, you must also unseem your attempts to do so. Follow it back and you must ultimately unseem yourself and the very World. This leads us to two conclusions. First: 
All objects are seemed objects. 
Were an object unseemed, you would not have seemed it. And had you not seemed it, how do you know of it? There is no world but that of experience. Second: 
A seemed object is seemed always. 
In other words, to eat the apple is always to have been eating it. Were it otherwise, it would not be as it is.   
Question 2
Next, we turn our attention to the seemer. 
Imagine if you will, every part of yourself swapped out slowly in the manner of Theseus's ship. Identical limbs, an identical brain--everything different but the same. Do you seem yourself to yourself, or are you Other? If you answer Other, you lie, for the Other would not know itself as such. If you answer Yourself, you lie, for there is evidently no inherent self-object to call your own. This leads us to a third conclusion: 
The seemer is not an object 
Which is to say, the Seemer is Subject. Subject to what? To seeming. And what is seeming? It is that which the Seemer does. The verb which proves the noun. Adam adams. The seemer seems. 
Question 3
We come now to our final thought experiment: to seem an object without reference to yourself as seemer. You go about it thusly: isolation of object in space, reduction to pieces, blocking-up of gaps between. 
But you find you cannot conceive the thing wholly--only this side or that. The seemed remains situated always toward and against the seemer. To eat the apple is to eat it in relation. We finally must declare that: 
The seemer seems itself always as seeming 
And what does this yield? Nothing and everything. Recall, objects are seemed always by the seemer, which is subject. However, in seeming, the seemer seems doubly (as in a mirror), rendering itself Subject-Object.  
What does it seem in the mirror of the World? Not itself, but itself seeming itself: an uncaused causing. In eating, Adam generates the object of his eating. Through experience, the self creates the World (paradise tainted).  
This at the last is Seeming Eternally Seemed: the apple and the man, the serpent and his tail.

Text:
ASTHMA
What is the god? How might we conceive of him? Not as the man, but as the fullness of the man and, thus, of the world.
What are the qualities of the world of the man? They are: a breathing, a becoming, a dreaming or a seeming.
With these, to propagate out and up is to imagine the god. We can, I think, breathe into the breathing to seem what it may become.
(Note that: this is not the GOD, or all things as they are. It is merely the god we seek, of some things as they might be.)
To the breathing. It is expansion and contraction, universally (the Big Bang or Shrink). I will call inhalation an eating and exhalation an excreting. This is System. The body breathes and is breathed through organs, tubes, and apertures. Man bellows - finds himself, above all, Lung.
To the becoming, Or, a going-toward-death. Not as the GOD goes--dumb and ultimate--but as the god, in glorious asphyxia. He unbreathes that he may become another's breath. This deliberate seizure of the death-function we call Becoming.
Finally to the seeming. Seeming is that operation by which breathing becomes. It is the breathing between the breath, tissue and filament of the god. To seem the breath: to feel it hot on the neck of the world. It is the great porous membrane, the passing-through. As like a dream as not, we seem its rise and fall, firmament upon firmament.
Take him for your wheezing god.

I swept the ash into an earthen vessel. 
Sometimes, I hear you chuckle at my notions.

Text:
APOTHEOSIS
Adoring the sacred skulls,
He thought where are their other bones?
     ribs
     metatarsals
Bleaching in the sun perhaps,
Or necklace of a sheik.
Black for white,
They once exchanged an ulcerated leg.
Anagyroi: unmercenary love.
One held aloft the unguent,
And one the stinking bottle.
Piss of the dying,
Piss of the radiant dead.
